title: Git Merging
description: Learn to merge branches, inspect merge history, abort merges, and resolve conflicts during integration.

tasks.prompt: Merge branch 'feature-auth' into the current branch.
tasks.hint: Use git merge followed by the branch name.
tasks.explanation: git merge integrates commits from another branch into HEAD.

tasks.prompt: Merge 'release' into the current branch, always creating a merge commit.
tasks.explanation: --no-ff preserves branch history with an explicit merge commit.

tasks.prompt: Abort an in-progress merge and restore the pre-merge state.
tasks.explanation: git merge --abort cancels conflict resolution and returns to the prior state.

tasks.prompt: Show commit history as a decorated graph with all branches.
tasks.explanation: The graph view visualizes merges and branch divergence.

tasks.prompt: Show details of the most recent merge commit.
tasks.explanation: git show --merge highlights changes introduced by a merge.

tasks.prompt: List files with unresolved merge conflicts.
Stage 'app.py' after manually resolving its merge conflict.
tasks.hint: Use git add on the resolved file.
tasks.explanation: Staging marks a conflict as resolved so the merge can complete.

tasks.prompt: Complete the merge with commit message 'Merge feature-auth into main'.
tasks.hint: Use git commit -m with a descriptive merge message.
tasks.explanation: After resolving conflicts, git commit finalizes the merge.
